{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|9.95|N|135|37|49.00|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kanayama Kofun'''|金山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Seryutani neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Kanan, Osaka|Kanan]], [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1991.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172575 |title=金山古墳 |trans-title=Kanayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kanayama Kofun is an extremely rare {{nihongo|''sōen-fun''|双円墳}}, which is shaped like a dumbbell, consisting of two joined circular tumuli. It is located in the innermost part of the Kanan Plateau at the western foot of [[:en:Mount Yamato Katsuragi|Mount Katsuragi]], in the southeastern part of Osaka Prefecture. The two circular tumuli are connected in parallel in the northwest–southeast axis. The north tumulus is a two-tiered construction with a diameter of 38.6 meters, the south tumulus is a three-tiered construction with a diameter of 55.4 meters, and the total length of the mound is 85.8 meters. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found, and ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on both the north and south tumuli, but only on the west side of the constriction, and stone rows are found on the third step of the southern tumulus. The ''kofun'' is surrounded by a double-circular moat with an average depth of 1.4 meters, giving a total length, including the moat, of 104 meters. [[Pa11y field]]s that are one step lower than the surrounding land surround the outer circumference of the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] in the northern tumulus is a large stone chamber with a total length of 10.6 meters, containing two house-shaped sarcophagi made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]], one in the main chamber and one in the entrance vestibule. It appears to have been robbed in the early 7th century, but [[archaeological excavation]]s have detected residual [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] including a few glass balls, silver rings, horse harnesses, iron swords and earthenware. Details of the burial chamber in the southern terminus are less clear as it has not been fully excavated, but it also appears to be a horizontal stone chamber. From the style of construction, is thought that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to legend, one of these tumuli was the grave of Prince Saegusa, one of the sons of [[:en:Prince Shotoku|Prince Shotoku]].
Archaeological excavations were carried out in 1946 and from 1993 to 1994. From1995, the site has been maintained as Kanayama Kofun Park. It is a three-minute walk from the "Seryutani" bus stop on the Kongo Bus from [[:en:Tondabayashi Station|Tondabayashi Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Nagano Line|Kintetsu Nagano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 85.8 meters: ;North Kofun: 38.6 meter diameter x 6.8 meters high, 2-tier ;South Kofun: 55.4 meter diameter x 9.4 meters high, 3-tier
==Gallery== <gallery> 750314 Kanayama kofun aerial.jpg|Aerial photograph Kanayama Kofun, kitakyu.jpg|North Kofun Kanayama Kofun, minamikyu.jpg|South Kofun Kanayama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|North Kofun burial chamber sarcophagus Kanayama Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|North Kofun vestibule sarcophagus </gallery>

The {{nihongo|'''Kanayama Kofun'''|金山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Seryutani neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|en|Kanan, Osaka|WD=Q2305508|lt=Kanan}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|en|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|WD=Q1347323|lt=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1991.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172575 |title=金山古墳 |trans-title=Kanayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kanayama Kofun is an extremely rare {{nihongo|''sōen-fun''|双円墳}}, which is shaped like a dumbbell, consisting of two joined circular tumuli. It is located in the innermost part of the Kanan Plateau at the western foot of {{ill|Mount Yamato Katsuragi|en|Mount Yamato Katsuragi|WD=Q11433560|lt=Mount Katsuragi}}, in the southeastern part of Osaka Prefecture. The two circular tumuli are connected in parallel in the northwest–southeast axis. The north tumulus is a two-tiered construction with a diameter of 38.6 meters, the south tumulus is a three-tiered construction with a diameter of 55.4 meters, and the total length of the mound is 85.8 meters. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found, and ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on both the north and south tumuli, but only on the west side of the constriction, and stone rows are found on the third step of the southern tumulus. The ''kofun'' is surrounded by a double-circular moat with an average depth of 1.4 meters, giving a total length, including the moat, of 104 meters. [[Pa11y field]]s that are one step lower than the surrounding land surround the outer circumference of the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] in the northern tumulus is a large stone chamber with a total length of 10.6 meters, containing two house-shaped sarcophagi made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}}, one in the main chamber and one in the entrance vestibule. It appears to have been robbed in the early 7th century, but [[archaeological excavation]]s have detected residual {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} including a few glass balls, silver rings, horse harnesses, iron swords and earthenware. Details of the burial chamber in the southern terminus are less clear as it has not been fully excavated, but it also appears to be a horizontal stone chamber. From the style of construction, is thought that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to legend, one of these tumuli was the grave of Prince Saegusa, one of the sons of [[:en:Prince Shotoku|Prince Shotoku]].
Archaeological excavations were carried out in 1946 and from 1993 to 1994. From1995, the site has been maintained as Kanayama Kofun Park. It is a three-minute walk from the "Seryutani" bus stop on the Kongo Bus from {{ill|Tondabayashi Station|en|Tondabayashi Station|WD=Q7820789|lt=Tondabayashi Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Nagano Line|Kintetsu Nagano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It is a three-minute walk from the "Seryutani" bus stop on the Kongo Bus from {{ill|Tondabayashi Station|en|Tondabayashi Station|WD=Q7820789|lt=Tondabayashi Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Nagano Line|Kintetsu Nagano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kanayama Kofun | native_name =金山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kanayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kanayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kanayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.469392|N|135.630331|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|en|Kanan, Osaka|WD=Q2305508|lt=Kanan, Osaka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|9.95|N|135|37|49.00|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kanayama Kofun'''|金山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Seryutani neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|en|Kanan, Osaka|WD=Q2305508|lt=Kanan}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|en|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|WD=Q1347323|lt=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1991.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172575 |title=金山古墳 |trans-title=Kanayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kanayama Kofun is an extremely rare {{nihongo|''sōen-fun''|双円墳}}, which is shaped like a dumbbell, consisting of two joined circular tumuli. It is located in the innermost part of the Kanan Plateau at the western foot of {{ill|Mount Yamato Katsuragi|en|Mount Yamato Katsuragi|WD=Q11433560|lt=Mount Katsuragi}}, in the southeastern part of Osaka Prefecture. The two circular tumuli are connected in parallel in the northwest–southeast axis. The north tumulus is a two-tiered construction with a diameter of 38.6 meters, the south tumulus is a three-tiered construction with a diameter of 55.4 meters, and the total length of the mound is 85.8 meters. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found, and ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on both the north and south tumuli, but only on the west side of the constriction, and stone rows are found on the third step of the southern tumulus. The ''kofun'' is surrounded by a double-circular moat with an average depth of 1.4 meters, giving a total length, including the moat, of 104 meters. [[Pa11y field]]s that are one step lower than the surrounding land surround the outer circumference of the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] in the northern tumulus is a large stone chamber with a total length of 10.6 meters, containing two house-shaped sarcophagi made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}}, one in the main chamber and one in the entrance vestibule. It appears to have been robbed in the early 7th century, but [[archaeological excavation]]s have detected residual {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} including a few glass balls, silver rings, horse harnesses, iron swords and earthenware. Details of the burial chamber in the southern terminus are less clear as it has not been fully excavated, but it also appears to be a horizontal stone chamber. From the style of construction, is thought that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to legend, one of these tumuli was the grave of Prince Saegusa, one of the sons of [[:en:Prince Shotoku|Prince Shotoku]].
Archaeological excavations were carried out in 1946 and from 1993 to 1994. From1995, the site has been maintained as Kanayama Kofun Park. It is a three-minute walk from the "Seryutani" bus stop on the Kongo Bus from {{ill|Tondabayashi Station|en|Tondabayashi Station|WD=Q7820789|lt=Tondabayashi Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Nagano Line|Kintetsu Nagano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 85.8 meters: ;North Kofun: 38.6 meter diameter x 6.8 meters high, 2-tier ;South Kofun: 55.4 meter diameter x 9.4 meters high, 3-tier

The {{nihongo|'''Kanayama Kofun'''|金山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Seryutani neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|en|Kanan, Osaka|WD=Q2305508|lt=Kanan}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|en|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|WD=Q1347323|lt=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1991.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172575 |title=金山古墳 |trans-title=Kanayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kanayama Kofun is an extremely rare {{nihongo|''sōen-fun''|双円墳}}, which is shaped like a dumbbell, consisting of two joined circular tumuli. It is located in the innermost part of the Kanan Plateau at the western foot of {{ill|Mount Yamato Katsuragi|en|Mount Yamato Katsuragi|WD=Q11433560|lt=Mount Katsuragi}}, in the southeastern part of Osaka Prefecture. The two circular tumuli are connected in parallel in the northwest–southeast axis. The north tumulus is a two-tiered construction with a diameter of 38.6 meters, the south tumulus is a three-tiered construction with a diameter of 55.4 meters, and the total length of the mound is 85.8 meters. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found, and ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on both the north and south tumuli, but only on the west side of the constriction, and stone rows are found on the third step of the southern tumulus. The ''kofun'' is surrounded by a double-circular moat with an average depth of 1.4 meters, giving a total length, including the moat, of 104 meters. [[Pa11y field]]s that are one step lower than the surrounding land surround the outer circumference of the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] in the northern tumulus is a large stone chamber with a total length of 10.6 meters, containing two house-shaped sarcophagi made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}}, one in the main chamber and one in the entrance vestibule. It appears to have been robbed in the early 7th century, but [[archaeological excavation]]s have detected residual {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} including a few glass balls, silver rings, horse harnesses, iron swords and earthenware. Details of the burial chamber in the southern terminus are less clear as it has not been fully excavated, but it also appears to be a horizontal stone chamber. From the style of construction, is thought that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to legend, one of these tumuli was the grave of Prince Saegusa, one of the sons of [[:en:Prince Shotoku|Prince Shotoku]].
Archaeological excavations were carried out in 1946 and from 1993 to 1994. From1995, the site has been maintained as Kanayama Kofun Park. It is a three-minute walk from the "Seryutani" bus stop on the Kongo Bus from {{ill|Tondabayashi Station|en|Tondabayashi Station|WD=Q7820789|lt=Tondabayashi Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Nagano Line|Kintetsu Nagano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 85.8 meters: ;North Kofun: 38.6 meter diameter x 6.8 meters high, 2-tier ;South Kofun: 55.4 meter diameter x 9.4 meters high, 3-tier
==Gallery== <gallery> 750314 Kanayama kofun aerial.jpg|Aerial photograph Kanayama Kofun, kitakyu.jpg|North Kofun Kanayama Kofun, minamikyu.jpg|South Kofun Kanayama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|North Kofun burial chamber sarcophagus Kanayama Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|North Kofun vestibule sarcophagus </gallery>

[[Pa11y field]]s that are one step lower than the surrounding land surround the outer circumference of the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] in the northern tumulus is a large stone chamber with a total length of 10.6 meters, containing two house-shaped sarcophagi made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}}, one in the main chamber and one in the entrance vestibule. It appears to have been robbed in the early 7th century, but [[archaeological excavation]]s have detected residual {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} including a few glass balls, silver rings, horse harnesses, iron swords and earthenware. Details of the burial chamber in the southern terminus are less clear as it has not been fully excavated, but it also appears to be a horizontal stone chamber. From the style of construction, is thought that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to legend, one of these tumuli was the grave of Prince Saegusa, one of the sons of [[:en:Prince Shotoku|Prince Shotoku]].
Archaeological excavations were carried out in 1946 and from 1993 to 1994. From1995, the site has been maintained as Kanayama Kofun Park. It is a three-minute walk from the "Seryutani" bus stop on the Kongo Bus from {{ill|Tondabayashi Station|en|Tondabayashi Station|WD=Q7820789|lt=Tondabayashi Station|1=Tondabayashi Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Nagano Line|Kintetsu Nagano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Kanayama Kofun'''|金山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Seryutani neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|en|Kanan, Osaka|WD=Q2305508|lt=Kanan|1=Kanan, Osaka}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|en|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|WD=Q1347323|lt=Minamikawachi District, Osaka|1=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1991.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172575 |title=金山古墳 |trans-title=Kanayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kanayama Kofun is an extremely rare {{nihongo|''sōen-fun''|双円墳}}, which is shaped like a dumbbell, consisting of two joined circular tumuli. It is located in the innermost part of the Kanan Plateau at the western foot of {{ill|Mount Yamato Katsuragi|en|Mount Yamato Katsuragi|WD=Q11433560|lt=Mount Katsuragi|1=Mount Yamato Katsuragi}}, in the southeastern part of Osaka Prefecture. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found, and ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on both the north and south tumuli, but only on the west side of the constriction, and stone rows are found on the third step of the southern tumulus. {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kanayama Kofun | native_name =金山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kanayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kanayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kanayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.469392|N|135.630331|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|en|Kanan, Osaka|qid=Q2305508|lt=Kanan, Osaka|1=Kanan, Osaka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|9.95|N|135|37|49.00|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kanayama Kofun'''|金山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Seryutani neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|en|Kanan, Osaka|qid=Q2305508|lt=Kanan|1=Kanan, Osaka}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|en|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|qid=Q1347323|lt=Minamikawachi District, Osaka|1=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1991.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172575 |title=金山古墳 |trans-title=Kanayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kanayama Kofun is an extremely rare {{nihongo|''sōen-fun''|双円墳}}, which is shaped like a dumbbell, consisting of two joined circular tumuli. It is located in the innermost part of the Kanan Plateau at the western foot of {{ill|Mount Yamato Katsuragi|en|Mount Yamato Katsuragi|qid=Q11433560|lt=Mount Katsuragi|1=Mount Yamato Katsuragi}}, in the southeastern part of Osaka Prefecture. The two circular tumuli are connected in parallel in the northwest–southeast axis. The north tumulus is a two-tiered construction with a diameter of 38.6 meters, the south tumulus is a three-tiered construction with a diameter of 55.4 meters, and the total length of the mound is 85.8 meters. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found, and ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on both the north and south tumuli, but only on the west side of the constriction, and stone rows are found on the third step of the southern tumulus. The ''kofun'' is surrounded by a double-circular moat with an average depth of 1.4 meters, giving a total length, including the moat, of 104 meters. [[Pa11y field]]s that are one step lower than the surrounding land surround the outer circumference of the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] in the northern tumulus is a large stone chamber with a total length of 10.6 meters, containing two house-shaped sarcophagi made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|qid=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}}, one in the main chamber and one in the entrance vestibule. It appears to have been robbed in the early 7th century, but [[archaeological excavation]]s have detected residual {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} including a few glass balls, silver rings, horse harnesses, iron swords and earthenware. Details of the burial chamber in the southern terminus are less clear as it has not been fully excavated, but it also appears to be a horizontal stone chamber. From the style of construction, is thought that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to legend, one of these tumuli was the grave of Prince Saegusa, one of the sons of [[:en:Prince Shotoku|Prince Shotoku]].
Archaeological excavations were carried out in 1946 and from 1993 to 1994. From1995, the site has been maintained as Kanayama Kofun Park. It is a three-minute walk from the "Seryutani" bus stop on the Kongo Bus from {{ill|Tondabayashi Station|en|Tondabayashi Station|qid=Q7820789|lt=Tondabayashi Station|1=Tondabayashi Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Nagano Line|Kintetsu Nagano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 85.8 meters: ;North Kofun: 38.6 meter diameter x 6.8 meters high, 2-tier ;South Kofun: 55.4 meter diameter x 9.4 meters high, 3-tier
